alaska.sched: Scheduled to rise to $14.00 on July 1, 2026
alaska.thresh: Daily/weekly premium not applicable to employers of fewer than 4 employees

arkansas.thresh: Applies to employers with 4 or more employees

california.ot: Daily over 8 (1.5x), over 12 (2x), weekly over 40, plus 7th-day rules
california.extra: Many California cities set higher local minimums

colorado.extra: Denver sets a higher local minimum

connecticut.extra: 7th consecutive day premium in restaurants and hotel restaurants

delaware.extra: State paid family and medical leave benefits begin in 2026

florida.sched: Rising to $15.00 on September 30, 2026 under the constitutional amendment schedule

georgia.thresh: State law applies to employers of 6 or more

hawaii.extra: Temporary disability insurance (TDI) coverage is required for employees

illinois.thresh: Applies to employers of 4 or more (family members excluded)
illinois.extra: Paid Leave for All Workers: leave usable for any reason

indiana.thresh: Applies to employers of 2 or more

kentucky.ot: Weekly over 40, plus a 7th-day overtime rule

maine.extra: Earned paid leave is usable for any reason

maryland.extra: State FAMLI paid leave program is enacted and phasing in; confirm current dates

massachusetts.extra: State rate must stay at least $0.50 above the federal rate

michigan.sched: Scheduled to reach $15.00 on January 1, 2027, then formula-adjusted
michigan.thresh: Applies to employers of 2 or more

minnesota.extra: Earned sick and safe time statewide; state paid leave program launching in 2026

missouri.extra: Voter-approved sick leave rules were modified by the legislature; verify current status with the state

montana.thresh: $4.00 rate allowed for non-FLSA businesses with gross annual sales of $110,000 or less

nebraska.thresh: Applies to employers of 4 or more
nebraska.extra: Voter-approved paid sick leave recently took effect; verify details

nevada.ot: Daily over 8 for employees earning under 1.5x minimum wage; weekly over 40 otherwise
nevada.extra: Paid leave usable for any reason at employers with 50 or more employees

new-hampshire.extra: Voluntary state paid family leave plan available

new-jersey.thresh: $15.23 rate for employers with fewer than 6 employees and seasonal work

new-mexico.extra: Santa Fe and Albuquerque set higher local minimums

new-york.w: $17.00 in NYC, Nassau, Suffolk, and Westchester; $16.00 rest of state
new-york.extra: Spread-of-hours and split-shift rules add an extra hour of pay in some cases

oklahoma.w: $7.25 (larger employers; $2.00 state rate otherwise, superseded by FLSA)

oregon.ot: Weekly over 40, plus daily rules in some mills and factories

pennsylvania.extra: Philadelphia and Pittsburgh have local paid sick leave ordinances

rhode-island.ot: Weekly over 40, plus Sunday and holiday premium rules in retail

vermont.thresh: Applies to employers of 2 or more
vermont.extra: Voluntary state paid family leave plan available

washington.extra: Seattle and other cities set higher local minimums

west-virginia.thresh: Applies to employers of 6 or more at one location
